idioms.name: use_using_disposable
idioms.description: Always use 'using' declarations or statements for IDisposable objects. Never manually call Dispose().
idioms.detection: Detect IDisposable objects created without using statements. Detect manual Dispose() calls in finally blocks.
idioms.fix: Wrap in using declaration (C# 8+) or using statement.
idioms.bad_example:
var stream = new FileStream(path, FileMode.Open);
try { ... } finally { stream.Dispose(); }
idioms.good_example: using var stream = new FileStream(path, FileMode.Open);

idioms.name: async_await_properly
idioms.description: Use async/await consistently. Never use .Result or .Wait() on tasks — they cause deadlocks. Always suffix async methods with Async.
idioms.detection: Detect .Result, .Wait(), .GetAwaiter().GetResult() calls on Task. Detect async void methods (except event handlers).
idioms.fix: Await the task. Make the calling method async. Use async Task not async void.

idioms.name: nullable_reference_types
idioms.description: Enable nullable reference types and annotate nullability. Check for null before dereferencing nullable types.
idioms.detection: Detect missing null checks on nullable parameters. Detect disabled nullable context.
idioms.fix: Enable <Nullable>enable</Nullable> in csproj. Annotate nullable params with ?. Use null-conditional and null-coalescing operators.
idioms.good_example: string GetName(User? user) { return user?.Name?.ToUpper() ?? string.Empty; }

idioms.name: use_pattern_matching
idioms.description: Use C# pattern matching (is, switch expressions, property patterns) instead of verbose type checks and casts.
idioms.detection: Detect (x is Type) followed by cast. Detect verbose switch statements that could be switch expressions.
idioms.fix: Use 'is Type t' pattern, switch expressions, property patterns.
idioms.bad_example: if (shape is Circle) { var c = (Circle)shape; return c.Radius; }
idioms.good_example: if (shape is Circle c) { return c.Radius; }

idioms.name: prefer_linq
idioms.description: Prefer LINQ for collection transformations over manual loops. Use method syntax for simple queries.
idioms.detection: Detect foreach loops building new lists via Add() that could be Select/Where/Aggregate.
idioms.fix: Replace with LINQ methods: Select, Where, Aggregate, Any, All, FirstOrDefault.
idioms.bad_example: var names = new List<string>(); foreach (var u in users) names.Add(u.Name);

idioms.name: use_dependency_injection
idioms.description: Use constructor injection for dependencies. Register services in DI container rather than newing up dependencies.
idioms.detection: Detect new ServiceClass() inside business logic. Detect static service locator patterns.
idioms.fix: Inject via constructor. Register in IServiceCollection. Use interfaces for mockability.
idioms.bad_example: public class OrderService { public void Process() { var db = new SqlDatabase(); } }
idioms.good_example: public class OrderService { private readonly IDatabase _db; public OrderService(IDatabase db) { _db = db; } }

idioms.name: use_records_for_data
idioms.description: Use record types for immutable data transfer objects. Records provide value equality and with-expressions.
idioms.detection: Detect classes with only auto-properties and no behaviour that could be records.
idioms.fix: Convert to record or record struct for value semantics.
idioms.bad_example: public class Point { public int X { get; set; } public int Y { get; set; } }
idioms.good_example: public record Point(int X, int Y);
